Title: Combine name plus gender plus age range in one query
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; at least 3 patients matching name fragment exist with differing gender and age
Test_Steps: 1. Open Find Patient advanced search | 2. Enter name fragment | 3. Select gender filter | 4. Set age range filter | 5. Execute search
Expected_Result: Only patients matching all three criteria simultaneously are returned; non-matching gender or out-of-range ages are excluded

Title: AND vs OR semantics across criteria via decision table
Preconditions: Advanced search supports configurable criteria combination
Test_Steps: 1. Apply criteria combinations per decision table | 2. Record returned set for each row | 3. Compare against expected AND/OR semantics
Test_Data: Combos: name+id; name only; id only; none
Expected_Result: Empty criteria returns validation prompt; single criterion narrows correctly; multiple criteria intersect (AND) not union; results match expected truth table

Title: Search by exact patient identifier
Preconditions: A patient with a known OpenMRS identifier exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Find Patient | 2. Enter the full identifier | 3. Execute search
Expected_Result: Exactly the matching patient is returned with correct demographics

Title: Identifier search with check-digit validation boundary
Preconditions: Identifier type uses Luhn or mod-30 check digit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter identifier with valid check digit | 2. Search | 3. Enter identifier with invalid check digit | 4. Search
Expected_Result: Valid identifier returns the patient; invalid check-digit identifier returns no patient or a check-digit warning, never a wrong patient

Title: Leading-zero and whitespace-padded identifier handling
Preconditions: A patient with identifier 0001234 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Search with leading zeros stripped | 2. Search with surrounding whitespace | 3. Search with internal spaces
Expected_Result: Whitespace is trimmed and match succeeds where appropriate; internal spaces do not silently match a different patient

Title: Old or retired identifier still resolves patient
Preconditions: A patient has a voided/retired prior identifier and a current identifier
Test_Steps: 1. Search using the retired identifier | 2. Observe result | 3. Search using current identifier
Expected_Result: Retired identifier resolves to the same patient or clearly indicates retirement; never resolves to an unrelated patient

Title: Partial name prefix search returns ranked matches
Preconditions: Multiple patients share a common name prefix
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a 3-character name prefix | 2. Execute search | 3. Review ranking
Expected_Result: All patients whose given or family name starts with the prefix appear; exact/closer matches rank higher

Title: Minimum search term length boundary
Preconditions: Search enforces a configured minimum character threshold
Test_Steps: 1. Enter 1 character and search | 2. Enter 2 characters | 3. Enter the configured minimum
Expected_Result: Sub-threshold input is rejected with guidance and no expensive query runs; threshold length executes normally

Title: Multi-token name search across given and family names
Preconditions: Patients exist with two-part names
Test_Steps: 1. Enter two name tokens separated by space | 2. Search
Expected_Result: Patients matching both tokens across name fields are returned; token order independence verified

Title: Soundex/Metaphone phonetic match for misspelled name
Preconditions: Phonetic matching configured (e.g., OpenMRS name search with soundex)
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a phonetically equivalent misspelling | 2. Search
Expected_Result: Phonetically similar patient is returned even though spelling differs

Title: Edit-distance tolerance for single-character typo
Preconditions: Fuzzy search enabled with a max edit distance
Test_Steps: 1. Enter name with one transposed character | 2. Search | 3. Enter name with three errors | 4. Search
Expected_Result: Single typo within tolerance returns match; excessive errors beyond threshold do not return spurious matches

Title: Phonetic false-positive guardrail
Preconditions: Two phonetically distinct patients exist
Test_Steps: 1. Search a name | 2. Confirm an unrelated distinct-sounding patient is not returned
Expected_Result: Distinct-sounding unrelated patient is excluded; phonetic matching does not over-broaden to unsafe degree

Title: Accent-insensitive name search
Preconditions: A patient stored with diacritics exists
Test_Steps: 1. Search using unaccented characters | 2. Search using accented characters
Expected_Result: Both accented and unaccented queries match the stored diacritic name per normalization rules

Title: Unicode normalization NFC vs NFD equivalence
Preconditions: Name stored with combining vs precomposed characters
Test_Steps: 1. Search precomposed form | 2. Search decomposed form
Expected_Result: Both Unicode normalization forms resolve to the same patient consistently

Title: Non-Latin script name search (CJK/Arabic)
Preconditions: Patient with non-Latin name stored
Test_Steps: 1. Enter non-Latin name | 2. Search | 3. Verify rendering of results
Expected_Result: Non-Latin name is matched and rendered without mojibake; right-to-left scripts display correctly

Title: Search by exact date of birth
Preconditions: Patients with known DOBs exist
Test_Steps: 1. Enter exact DOB | 2. Combine with name fragment | 3. Search
Expected_Result: Patients matching the exact DOB (and name) are returned; locale date format respected

Title: Estimated/approximate DOB and birth-year-only search
Preconditions: A patient with estimated DOB (birthdateEstimated) exists
Test_Steps: 1. Search by birth year only | 2. Search by estimated age
Expected_Result: Estimated-DOB patients are included appropriately; age computed from estimated birthdate is consistent

Title: Invalid and future DOB rejection
Preconditions: Advanced search has DOB input
Test_Steps: 1. Enter future DOB | 2. Enter Feb 30 | 3. Enter malformed date
Expected_Result: Each invalid DOB is rejected with a clear message; no query executes against malformed input

Title: Search by person attribute (phone number)
Preconditions: Phone number person attribute is searchable and a patient has one
Test_Steps: 1. Enter phone number | 2. Search
Expected_Result: Patient with matching phone attribute is returned; partial phone match behaves per configuration

Title: Search by alternate identifier types via pairwise
Preconditions: Multiple identifier types configured (MRN, national ID, insurance)
Test_Steps: 1. Run pairwise combinations of identifier type and value validity | 2. Record outcomes
Expected_Result: Each identifier-type and validity pair behaves correctly; type-scoped search does not cross-match other types

Title: Sort results by name ascending and descending
Preconditions: Search returns multiple results
Test_Steps: 1. Execute a broad search | 2. Sort by name ascending | 3. Sort descending
Expected_Result: Result order toggles correctly and stably; locale-aware collation applied

Title: Sort by DOB and by identifier with tie-breaking
Preconditions: Results contain ties on a sort key
Test_Steps: 1. Sort by DOB | 2. Sort by identifier | 3. Inspect tie ordering
Expected_Result: Primary sort applied correctly; deterministic secondary tie-breaker keeps order stable across reloads

Title: Result set capped at configured maximum
Preconditions: A query matches more than the configured result cap
Test_Steps: 1. Execute a query matching > cap rows | 2. Inspect returned count and indicator
Expected_Result: Returned rows do not exceed the cap; a truncation/too-many indicator prompts the user to refine

Title: Pagination across result pages preserves criteria
Preconditions: Query returns multiple pages
Test_Steps: 1. Execute search | 2. Go to page 2 | 3. Go to last page | 4. Return to page 1 | 5. Change a filter
Expected_Result: Criteria persist across pages; no duplicate or skipped rows at page boundaries; changing a filter resets to page 1

Title: Zero-result query shows guidance not error
Preconditions: A query guaranteed to match nothing
Test_Steps: 1. Search an impossible value | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: Empty-state message with refine/register suggestions shown; no stack trace or raw error

Title: Overly broad query throttled with refine prompt
Preconditions: A single-letter or wildcard query could match thousands
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an extremely broad term | 2. Search
Expected_Result: System prevents unbounded retrieval, returns capped set with a clear refine-your-search prompt; no UI freeze

Title: Create and re-run a saved search
Preconditions: Saved/named search feature available (Platform/adapter)
Test_Steps: 1. Build a multi-criteria search | 2. Save it with a name | 3. Re-open and run it
Expected_Result: Saved search persists criteria and reproduces the same logical result set on re-run

Title: Saved search visibility scoped to owner/role
Preconditions: Two users with different roles; one saved search owned by user A
Test_Steps: 1. As user A save a search | 2. As user B attempt to view/run it | 3. Verify scope
Expected_Result: Saved search is visible only per its sharing scope; user B cannot access a private saved search

Title: Search latency at high patient volume
Preconditions: Dataset seeded with large patient population
Test_Steps: 1. Execute typical name search | 2. Measure response time | 3. Repeat under concurrent load
Expected_Result: Response time stays within SLA threshold; latency does not degrade non-linearly; no timeouts

Title: Index usage verified for identifier and name search
Preconditions: DB access to query plans
Test_Steps: 1. Capture query plan for identifier search | 2. Capture plan for name search | 3. Inspect index usage
Expected_Result: Queries use appropriate indexes (no full table scans on hot paths); plans confirm index seeks

Title: SQL injection attempt in search field
Preconditions: Search input fields accessible
Test_Steps: 1. Enter SQL meta-characters in name field | 2. Enter injection payload in identifier | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Input is parameterized/escaped; no data leak, no error disclosure, no query manipulation; safe empty/no-result returned

Title: Stored/reflected XSS via search term and result rendering
Preconditions: Search term may be echoed in results header
Test_Steps: 1. Enter script payload as search term | 2. Submit | 3. Inspect rendered result page
Expected_Result: Payload is encoded/escaped on output; no script execution; CSP not violated

Title: Search results respect patient-level access control
Preconditions: A restricted/confidential patient exists; user lacks access
Test_Steps: 1. Search for the restricted patient as unauthorized user | 2. Observe results
Expected_Result: Restricted patient is excluded or masked per access policy; existence not leaked via result count or autocomplete

Title: Patient search activity is audited
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled (e.g., OpenMRS logging/atomfeed or audit module)
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a patient search | 2. Open a patient from results | 3. Inspect audit log
Expected_Result: Search and subsequent record access are recorded with user, timestamp, criteria/patient; supports break-the-glass review

Title: Patient search by name parameter via FHIR
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?name=Smith | 2. Inspect Bundle
Expected_Result: Returns a searchset Bundle of matching Patient resources with correct total and self link

Title: FHIR identifier search with system|value token
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint with identifier system configured
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?identifier=system|value | 2. Inspect Bundle
Expected_Result: Only the patient with the matching identifier system and value is returned; system mismatch yields empty bundle

Title: FHIR birthdate range search with prefixes
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint supports date prefixes
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?birthdate=ge1980-01-01&birthdate=le1989-12-31 | 2. Inspect Bundle
Expected_Result: Patients within the inclusive date range returned; ge/le prefixes honored; out-of-range excluded

Title: FHIR _count and pagination links
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint returns paged bundles
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?name=a&_count=5 | 2. Follow next link | 3. Verify continuity
Expected_Result: Bundle honors _count; next/previous relation links work; no duplicates across pages; total accurate

Title: FHIR modifier :exact vs default contains behavior
Preconditions: FHIR string search supports :exact
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?family=Lee | 2. GET /Patient?family:exact=Lee
Expected_Result: Default performs starts-with/contains per server; :exact returns only exact matches; semantics documented and consistent

Title: FHIR invalid search parameter handling
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?bogusparam=x | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns OperationOutcome or ignores unknown param per policy without 500; behavior documented

Title: PDQ/QBP patient demographics query returns matches
Preconditions: HL7 v2 PDQ (QBP^Q22/RSP^K22) interface available
Test_Steps: 1. Send QBP^Q22 with name and DOB | 2. Inspect RSP^K22
Expected_Result: RSP returns candidate patients in QRI/PID segments with correct demographics and match confidence

Title: PIX cross-reference query resolves identifiers
Preconditions: HL7 PIX (QBP^Q23) interface available
Test_Steps: 1. Send QBP^Q23 with a known identifier and target domain | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Cross-referenced identifier(s) in the target domain are returned; unknown identifier yields empty/AE response

Title: PDQ no-match returns proper empty response
Preconditions: PDQ interface available
Test_Steps: 1. Send QBP^Q22 with impossible criteria | 2. Inspect RSP
Expected_Result: RSP indicates no data found (e.g., QAK with NF) without error; query acknowledged correctly

Title: Search form keyboard-only operation
Preconditions: Screen reader and keyboard available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through search fields | 2. Submit via Enter | 3. Navigate results with keyboard
Expected_Result: All fields reachable in logical order; focus visible; results navigable and announced; no keyboard trap

Title: Result count and no-result state announced to screen reader
Preconditions: Screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a search returning results | 2. Perform a search returning none | 3. Listen to announcements
Expected_Result: Live region announces result count and empty state; status changes are perceivable without visual cues

Title: Clearing one filter updates result set without full reload
Preconditions: Multi-criteria search executed with results shown
Test_Steps: 1. Apply name + gender + age filters | 2. Remove gender filter | 3. Remove age filter | 4. Clear all
Expected_Result: Each filter removal re-broadens results consistently; clear-all resets to initial state; no stale results persist

Title: Apostrophe and hyphen in names handled
Preconditions: Patients with names containing apostrophes/hyphens exist
Test_Steps: 1. Search O'Brien | 2. Search Smith-Jones | 3. Search without punctuation
Expected_Result: Punctuated names match with and without punctuation per normalization; apostrophe not treated as SQL delimiter

Title: Pairwise of match-mode and field combinations
Preconditions: Search supports exact and phonetic modes across name fields
Test_Steps: 1. Run pairwise combos of mode (exact/phonetic) and field (given/family) | 2. Record results
Expected_Result: Each mode-field pair returns the semantically correct set; modes do not bleed across fields unexpectedly

Title: Age vs DOB filter equivalence and off-by-one at year boundary
Preconditions: A patient whose birthday is today exists
Test_Steps: 1. Search by computed age | 2. Search by DOB | 3. Test patient with birthday today and yesterday
Expected_Result: Age computed correctly with no off-by-one at the birthday boundary; age filter and DOB filter agree

Title: Exploratory session on mixed criteria and rapid edits
Preconditions: Tester familiar with search workflows
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly add/remove/edit criteria | 2. Submit overlapping searches | 3. Resize/zoom; paste long input | 4. Observe anomalies
Expected_Result: No crashes, stale results, race conditions or layout breakage; anomalies logged with repro steps

Title: Concurrent identical searches return consistent results
Preconditions: Multiple sessions available
Test_Steps: 1. Issue same query from two sessions simultaneously | 2. Compare result sets
Expected_Result: Both sessions return identical, consistent result sets; no read anomalies under concurrency

Title: Search by address fields (city/state/postal)
Preconditions: Address-based search supported via adapter
Test_Steps: 1. Enter city filter | 2. Add postal code | 3. Search
Expected_Result: Patients with matching address attributes returned; combined address filters intersect correctly

Title: Search autocomplete does not leak data via timing or count
Preconditions: Type-ahead/autocomplete enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Type partial restricted-patient name | 2. Observe suggestions and response timing
Expected_Result: Autocomplete excludes unauthorized patients; no existence inference via suggestion presence or response timing

Title: FHIR _sort and combined parameter search
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint supports _sort
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?name=a&_sort=family&_count=10 | 2. Inspect order
Expected_Result: Results sorted by family name per spec; combined search and sort and count parameters interact correctly
